title: Acne Treatment
tagline: Clinically Proven Solutions for Clearer, Healthier Skin
quickSummary: Acne is a chronic skin condition affecting hair follicles and sebaceous glands — not just a teenage problem. Dr. Bangalee offers comprehensive in-clinic and home-care acne management programmes tailored to your skin type, severity, and goals.
priceFrom: R 850 – R 2,500 per session (package pricing available)
downtime: Minimal — 24–48 hours mild redness or peeling post-peel treatment
downtimeDetail: Chemical peel treatments: mild peeling, redness, and sensitivity for 2–5 days. Prescription skincare: some initial dryness or irritation as skin adapts. Dermapen (for acne scarring): 24–48 hours redness, 3–5 days minor social downtime.
suitableFor: Teenagers and young adults with persistent or severe breakoutsAdult women experiencing hormonal acne — breakouts that worsen around menstruation or with hormonal changesAnyone whose acne has not responded adequately to over-the-counter productsThose dealing with post-acne scarring, dark marks (post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation), or pittingPeople wanting a professional medical assessment to understand their specific type of acne and the most appropriate treatment
heroText: Struggling with breakouts, cysts, or post-acne dark marks? You are not alone — and more importantly, you don't have to manage acne alone. At Star Aesthetic Medical Centre, Dr. Rajeev Bangalee provides clinically proven, personalised acne treatment plans that go beyond standard over-the-counter products to address the root cause of your specific acne.
whatIs: Acne Vulgaris is a chronic inflammatory condition of the pilosebaceous unit — the hair follicle and its associated oil gland. When excess sebum production combines with dead skin cell build-up inside the pore, it creates the perfect environment for Cutibacterium acnes (the acne-causing bacterium) to proliferate, triggering the inflammation that produces pimples, papules, pustules, nodules, and cysts.At Star Aesthetic, Dr. Bangalee treats every type of acne — from mild comedonal acne (blackheads and whiteheads) and inflammatory acne (papules and pustules) to severe nodulo-cystic acne and hormonal acne in adult women. Treatment is always clinical, always personalised, and always designed to address the underlying cause rather than just the surface symptom.Acne is not caused by poor hygiene, eating chocolate, or laziness. It is a medical condition with genetic, hormonal, and environmental drivers — and it deserves medical treatment.
howWorks: Comprehensive Skin Assessment — Dr. Bangalee examines your skin, reviews your history (diet, stress, hormonal factors, previous treatments), and classifies your acne type and gradePersonalised Treatment Plan — A customised protocol is designed, combining in-clinic treatments with a medically tailored home-care regimenIn-Clinic Treatment — Depending on acne severity: chemical peels (salicylic acid, glycolic acid, or mesoestetic mesopeel), Dermapen microneedling for acne scarring, or mesoestetic acnelan treatmentHome-Care Programme — Pharmaceutical-grade skincare prescribed from our range: blemiderm® resurfacing gel, mesoprotech® SPF, mesoestetic Silymarin CF, or NeoStrata Clarify depending on your skin typePrescription Medication if Required — Topical or oral prescription treatments may be recommended for moderate-to-severe or antibiotic-resistant casesFollow-Up & Adjustment — Review appointments monitor progress and adjust the protocol as the skin responds
expectedResults: Weeks 1–2: Skin may experience initial purging (a temporary increase in breakouts as clogged pores clear) — this is expected and normal. Weeks 3–4: Reduction in new breakouts; improved skin texture. Weeks 6–8: Significantly clearer skin; reduction in existing dark marks. Weeks 10–12: Substantial improvement in skin clarity; ongoing fading of post-acne pigmentation.

faqs.question: What is the best acne treatment in Durban?
faqs.answer: There is no single 'best' treatment — effective acne management depends entirely on your specific acne type, skin type, severity, and contributing factors. At Star Aesthetic Medical Centre, Dr. Bangalee conducts a thorough assessment and creates a personalised plan that typically combines in-clinic treatments (chemical peels, microneedling) with pharmaceutical-grade home-care skincare. Book a consultation to receive your personalised recommendation.

faqs.question: Can adults get acne treatment — or is this just for teenagers?
faqs.answer: Absolutely — adult acne is extremely common, particularly in women, and is one of the most frequent concerns we see in the practice. Hormonal acne in adult women — which often presents along the jaw, chin, and neck — requires specific treatment approaches different from teenage acne. Dr. Bangalee regularly treats patients in their 30s, 40s, and 50s with ongoing acne concerns.

faqs.question: How many treatments will I need?
faqs.answer: Most patients see meaningful improvement within 3–6 in-clinic sessions, combined with consistent daily home-care skincare. More severe or cystic acne may require a longer programme. Dr. Bangalee will give you a clear expectation at your first consultation.

faqs.question: Will my acne come back after treatment?
faqs.answer: Acne has a tendency to recur, particularly if underlying triggers (hormonal fluctuations, stress, diet) are not addressed. However, with the right skincare programme and periodic maintenance visits, most patients maintain clear skin long-term. We will equip you with the knowledge and tools to manage any future flares quickly.

faqs.question: Is the treatment painful?
faqs.answer: Chemical peels cause mild tingling or warmth — most patients tolerate them very well. Dermapen microneedling uses topical anaesthetic and is described as a mild vibration sensation. In-clinic acne treatments are designed to be comfortable, not painful.

faqs.question: What causes adult hormonal acne?
faqs.answer: Hormonal acne in adult women is primarily driven by fluctuations in androgens (testosterone-like hormones) that stimulate excess sebum production. Common triggers include menstrual cycle fluctuations, perimenopause, stopping or starting hormonal contraception, polycystic ovarian syndrome (PCOS), and stress (which elevates cortisol, which in turn raises androgen levels).

faqs.question: What is the difference between acne and rosacea?
faqs.answer: Acne and rosacea can look similar but are distinct conditions. Acne involves clogged pores, comedones (blackheads/whiteheads), and bacterial-driven inflammation. Rosacea is a vascular condition with redness, visible blood vessels, and papules but no comedones. Treatment approaches are different — a professional assessment is essential to distinguish between the two and prescribe the correct treatment.

faqs.question: Can you treat acne scarring as well?
faqs.answer: Yes. Acne scarring — including icepick scars, boxcar scars, rolling scars, and post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (dark marks) — can be significantly improved through Dermapen microneedling, chemical peels, and mesoestetic professional treatments. We treat both active acne and its aftermath.

faqs.question: What skincare products should I use for acne-prone skin?
faqs.answer: The right products depend on your skin type and acne severity. We recommend pharmaceutical-grade cosmeceuticals from our shop range: mesoestetic blemiderm® Resurfacing Gel (evening treatment), SkinCeuticals Silymarin CF (morning antioxidant for oily skin), and mesoprotech® mineral fluid SPF50+ (daily sun protection). A complete protocol is prescribed at consultation.

faqs.question: Do I need to see Dr. Bangalee in person or can I get a skincare protocol online?
faqs.answer: A proper acne assessment requires an in-person consultation to evaluate your skin directly. However, if you are in Durban North or surrounding KZN areas, we welcome you at Star Aesthetic Medical Centre. Contact us at +27 (0)31 573 1325 or info@staraesthetic.site to book.

faqs.question: Will acne treatment help with the dark marks my spots leave behind?
faqs.answer: Yes — post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (the dark marks left after spots heal) is addressed as part of our acne management programme. Chemical peels and the melan tran3x home-care range are particularly effective for these marks.

faqs.question: What should I avoid eating to help my acne?
faqs.answer: The evidence base for dietary acne triggers is strongest for high-glycaemic index foods (white bread, sugary drinks, white rice) and dairy products in some individuals. These can spike insulin and IGF-1, which stimulates sebum production. However, diet alone is rarely sufficient to clear moderate or severe acne — medical treatment is the primary approach.

title: Body Contouring
tagline: Non-Surgical Fat Reduction & Body Sculpting in Durban
quickSummary: Body contouring uses advanced non-invasive technology to selectively reduce localised fat deposits and tighten skin — without surgery, without general anaesthesia, and without extended recovery periods.
priceFrom: R 1,200 – R 3,500 per session (area and modality dependent)
downtime: Minimal — return to normal activities immediately
downtimeDetail: Zero clinical downtime — this is one of the primary advantages of non-surgical body contouring over surgical liposuction. Mild redness, warmth, or temporary numbness in the treated area may occur immediately post-treatment and resolves within hours.
suitableFor: Adults at or near their healthy body weight with specific, localised fat depositsThose who have achieved diet and exercise goals but still have stubborn 'problem areas'Men and women wanting body sculpting without surgical downtimePatients preparing for or recovering from major life events — weddings, milestone birthdaysThose seeking a non-surgical alternative to liposuction
heroText: Frustrated with stubborn fat that refuses to respond to diet and exercise? Body contouring at Star Aesthetic Medical Centre offers non-surgical, clinically proven fat reduction and body sculpting treatments — targeting those problem areas that lifestyle changes alone cannot reach.
whatIs: Body contouring is the collective term for non-surgical aesthetic procedures designed to reduce localised fat deposits, tighten skin, and sculpt specific areas of the body. At Star Aesthetic, our body contouring treatments target the stubborn fat pockets that are genetically resistant to diet and exercise — the love handles, lower abdominal pouch, inner thighs, upper arms, and submental (under-chin) fat.Non-surgical body contouring is not a weight loss treatment — it is a precision body sculpting procedure for patients who are at or near their ideal weight but have localised fat areas they wish to reduce. It is the medical alternative to liposuction for patients who want meaningful fat reduction without surgery.
howWorks: Consultation & Body Analysis — Dr. Bangalee assesses your specific areas of concern, skin quality, and overall health to determine the most appropriate contouring modalityTreatment Mapping — Target areas are precisely mapped and photographed to track progressIn-Clinic Treatment — Advanced body contouring technology is applied to target areas. The treatment selectively acts on fat cells while leaving surrounding tissue unaffectedFat Cell Elimination — Treated fat cells undergo a natural metabolic elimination process, gradually reducing the fat layer over 4–12 weeksProgressive Sessions — Multiple sessions are typically required for optimal results, spaced 2–4 weeks apartResults Assessment — Progress photographs at each visit track measurable improvements in circumference and body shape
expectedResults: Weeks 2–4: Subtle early changes may be visible. Weeks 6–8: Noticeable reduction in the treated area. Weeks 10–12: Optimal results visible as treated fat cells are fully eliminated. Ongoing improvement may occur for up to 6 months post final session.

faqs.question: What is the difference between body contouring and weight loss?
faqs.answer: Body contouring reduces localised fat deposits in specific areas — it does not produce overall weight loss. It is most effective for patients at or near their goal weight who have stubborn areas that don't respond to diet and exercise. If you have significant weight to lose, we recommend achieving your weight loss goals first, then using body contouring to refine the result.

faqs.question: Is non-surgical body contouring as effective as liposuction?
faqs.answer: For the right candidate, non-surgical body contouring delivers excellent, measurable results without any surgical risk, anaesthesia, or extended recovery. Liposuction typically delivers more dramatic single-treatment results but comes with surgical risks and recovery time. Dr. Bangalee will advise which approach best suits your goals and starting point.

faqs.question: Does body contouring hurt?
faqs.answer: No — non-surgical body contouring treatments are designed to be comfortable. Most patients experience a warming sensation or mild suction pressure. No anaesthesia or recovery is required.

faqs.question: How many sessions do I need?
faqs.answer: Most patients require 4–8 sessions for optimal results, depending on the area and the degree of fat reduction desired. Dr. Bangalee will give you a clear treatment plan with expected number of sessions at your initial consultation.

faqs.question: Will the fat come back after treatment?
faqs.answer: Fat cells that are eliminated by body contouring do not regenerate — those cells are permanently gone. However, remaining fat cells in the body can enlarge with weight gain. Maintaining a healthy lifestyle protects and maximises your results.

faqs.question: Can body contouring help with loose skin?
faqs.answer: Depending on the degree of skin laxity and the technology used, some body contouring treatments also provide a skin tightening effect. Dr. Bangalee will assess your skin elasticity at consultation and recommend the most appropriate approach.

faqs.question: Who is NOT a good candidate for body contouring?
faqs.answer: Body contouring is not appropriate for pregnant or breastfeeding women, those with certain medical conditions (including active infections or implanted devices in the treatment area), or those with unrealistic expectations about outcomes. A thorough medical assessment at consultation determines candidacy.

faqs.question: Can I use body contouring to lose belly fat?
faqs.answer: Yes — the abdomen is one of the most commonly treated areas. Non-surgical body contouring can meaningfully reduce the lower abdominal pouch and flanks (love handles). Results vary by individual, and multiple sessions are typically needed for significant abdominal fat reduction.

faqs.question: How long before I see results?
faqs.answer: Early subtle changes appear from 4–6 weeks after your first session. Optimal results are typically visible at 10–12 weeks after your final session, as the body's natural elimination process completes.

faqs.question: Is body contouring safe?
faqs.answer: Non-surgical body contouring technologies used at Star Aesthetic are clinically validated and have extensive safety records. Dr. Bangalee conducts a full medical assessment before any treatment to ensure your safety and suitability.

title: Botox® Wrinkle Treatment
tagline: Natural-Looking Wrinkle Relaxation by Durban's Trusted Aesthetic Doctor
quickSummary: Botox (Botulinum Toxin Type A) is the world's most popular non-surgical cosmetic treatment — a purified protein that temporarily relaxes the facial muscles responsible for expression lines, delivering a smoother, younger-looking complexion with zero downtime.
downtime: Zero downtime — return to normal activities immediately
downtimeDetail: Zero clinical downtime. You can return to work, driving, and most normal activities immediately. Avoid strenuous exercise, alcohol, facial massage, and lying face-down for 24 hours post-treatment.
suitableFor: Women and men 25+ with forehead lines, frown lines, or crow's feet they wish to softenThose wanting a preventive approach — starting Botox before lines become deeply etchedPatients who want a non-surgical, zero-downtime solution for facial rejuvenationThose with excessive sweating (hyperhidrosis) in underarms, hands, or feetPatients looking to complement their SkinCeuticals P-TIOX home-care with in-clinic injectable support
heroText: Fine lines and expression wrinkles don't have to define how you look or feel. Botox at Star Aesthetic Medical Centre is administered exclusively by Dr. Rajeev Bangalee — an experienced aesthetic physician whose philosophy is natural-looking results, not a 'frozen' appearance. Look refreshed, rested, and like the best version of yourself.
whatIs: Botox (Botulinum Toxin Type A — marketed as Botox®, Dysport®, or Xeomin®) is a purified neurotoxin protein that temporarily blocks the nerve signal to specific facial muscles, causing them to relax. When the muscles responsible for creating expression lines (forehead creases, frown lines, crow's feet) are temporarily relaxed, the overlying skin smooths out and looks younger and more rested.Botox does not fill lines — it relaxes the muscles that create them. This is what makes it the ideal treatment for dynamic wrinkles (lines caused by movement) as opposed to static wrinkles (lines present at rest), which are better addressed with dermal fillers.At Star Aesthetic Medical Centre, every Botox treatment is administered personally by Dr. Bangalee using precise anatomical knowledge developed over years of aesthetic practice. His philosophy is always natural — subtle enhancement that makes you look refreshed, not overdone.
howWorks: Consultation — Dr. Bangalee discusses your concerns, assesses your facial anatomy, and creates a precise treatment plan. For first-time patients, photographs are taken for comparisonPreparation — The treatment area is cleansed. Topical anaesthetic cream is optional for sensitive patients but rarely required for BotoxInjection — Using a very fine needle, precise amounts of Botulinum Toxin are injected at specific anatomical points. The injections take 5–15 minutesPost-Treatment Care — You will receive written aftercare instructions. Sit upright for 4 hours; avoid strenuous exercise, saunas, and lying face-down for 24 hoursOnset — Results begin appearing within 3–5 days as the toxin takes effect2-Week Review — A complimentary review appointment at 2 weeks allows Dr. Bangalee to assess the result and make any fine-tuning adjustments
expectedResults: Day 3–5: Muscles begin to relax and initial softening of lines is visible. Day 10–14: Full effect is visible — this is the standard point at which results are evaluated. Duration: 3–5 months, depending on metabolism, muscle activity, and units used. With consistent treatment every 3–4 months, some patients find they need less product over time as muscles weaken with regular relaxation.

faqs.question: How long does Botox last?
faqs.answer: Botox results typically last 3–5 months. First-time patients often find it lasts closer to 3 months; with regular repeat treatments every 3–4 months, duration can extend to 5 months or longer as the targeted muscles weaken and require less product to maintain relaxation.

faqs.question: Will Botox make me look frozen or unnatural?
faqs.answer: Not when performed by an experienced aesthetic physician. Dr. Bangalee's philosophy is always natural — using conservative dosing and precise anatomical placement to soften lines while preserving natural facial expression. 'Frozen face' is the result of excessive dosing and poor technique, not Botox itself.

faqs.question: Does Botox hurt?
faqs.answer: The needle used is extremely fine (30–32 gauge — similar to an insulin needle) and injections are very brief. Most patients describe a mild sting lasting 1–2 seconds. The treatment is well-tolerated without anaesthetic for most patients.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Botox and fillers?
faqs.answer: Botox relaxes the muscles that create dynamic expression lines (forehead, frown, crow's feet). Dermal fillers restore volume, lift, and contour — addressing static lines, volume loss, and lip enhancement. The two treatments are frequently combined for comprehensive facial rejuvenation.

faqs.question: At what age should I start Botox?
faqs.answer: There is no single 'right age.' Some patients start in their late 20s as a preventive measure — using Botox to prevent deeply etched lines from forming. Most patients begin in their 30s or 40s when expression lines start to bother them at rest. Dr. Bangalee will advise the right timing based on your individual anatomy and concerns.

faqs.question: Is Botox safe?
faqs.answer: Yes. Botulinum Toxin has one of the most extensive safety records in medicine — it has been used clinically for over 30 years and is approved by both the FDA and SAHPRA. Administered by a qualified and experienced aesthetic physician like Dr. Bangalee, serious complications are exceptionally rare.

faqs.question: Can Botox be used to stop excessive sweating?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is one of Botox's most effective and life-changing applications. Injected into the underarms, hands, or feet, Botox blocks the nerve signals to sweat glands, dramatically reducing sweating for 6–9 months. Many patients consider this their most impactful treatment.

faqs.question: Can I exercise after Botox?
faqs.answer: Avoid strenuous exercise for 24 hours post-treatment. Exercise increases blood flow and can cause the toxin to migrate from the injection site before it has fully localised, potentially affecting unintended muscles.

faqs.question: Can men have Botox?
faqs.answer: Absolutely — and male Botox is increasingly popular. Men typically require higher doses due to stronger facial muscles and may need slightly different injection points to preserve a naturally masculine appearance. Dr. Bangalee treats male patients regularly.

faqs.question: What is the SkinCeuticals P-TIOX serum — can I use it instead of Botox?
faqs.answer: P-TIOX is a peptide serum inspired by the science of Botox — it reduces the appearance of expression lines topically. It is not as immediately dramatic as injectable Botox, but it delivers real results and is excellent for patients who want to extend their Botox results or who prefer a non-injectable approach. Dr. Bangalee can advise which is right for your goals.

faqs.question: How do I find a qualified Botox doctor in Durban?
faqs.answer: Always seek Botox treatment from a qualified, registered medical practitioner — not a beauty salon or untrained injector. Dr. Rajeev Bangalee is a qualified medical doctor registered with the HPCSA (Health Professions Council of South Africa) with specific aesthetic medicine training. Book at Star Aesthetic Medical Centre on +27 (0)31 573 1325.

title: Dermapen® Microneedling Treatment
tagline: Clinically Proven Skin Rejuvenation — Stimulating Your Skin's Own Collagen
quickSummary: Dermapen microneedling creates thousands of micro-channels in the skin, triggering a controlled healing response that stimulates collagen and elastin production. The result: visibly improved texture, reduced scarring, tighter pores, and a younger-looking complexion.
downtimeDetail: Social downtime of 24–48 hours due to redness. Makeup can typically be applied from day 3 post-treatment. Avoid direct sun exposure, swimming, and strenuous exercise for 48–72 hours. No saunas for 7 days.
suitableFor: Women and men with acne scarring — pitting, icepick scars, rolling scars, or boxcar scarsThose with enlarged pores, rough skin texture, or generally dull, tired-looking skinPatients with fine lines and early signs of skin ageing wanting a non-injectable rejuvenationThose with stretch marks (body) or skin laxity post-weight lossPatients with hair loss (scalp microneedling with growth factors)Anyone wanting to maximise the absorption of their home-care skincare products (microneedling dramatically improves product penetration)
heroText: Dermapen microneedling is one of the most versatile and effective skin rejuvenation treatments available — addressing everything from fine lines and acne scarring to enlarged pores and skin texture. At Star Aesthetic, Dr. Bangalee uses the authentic Dermapen® — the world's leading medical-grade microneedling device.
whatIs: Dermapen® microneedling is a minimally invasive skin rejuvenation procedure that uses a medical-grade device with multiple ultra-fine needles to create thousands of microscopic punctures in the skin's surface. These controlled micro-injuries trigger the skin's natural wound-healing response — stimulating the production of new collagen and elastin fibres that replace damaged or aged skin tissue.Unlike ablative laser treatments that remove the outer skin layer, microneedling stimulates regeneration from within while keeping the surface mostly intact. This means more effective healing, less risk of pigmentation changes (important for darker South African skin tones), and a significantly faster recovery than ablative alternatives.Dermapen® is the authentic, globally recognised gold standard in microneedling devices — used by aesthetic physicians, dermatologists, and plastic surgeons worldwide. At Star Aesthetic, every treatment is performed by Dr. Bangalee personally.
howWorks: Consultation & Skin Assessment — Dr. Bangalee assesses your skin concerns, type, and treatment history to design your Dermapen protocol and select appropriate serums for infusion during treatmentTopical Anaesthetic Application — EMLA cream is applied and left for 30–45 minutes to ensure your comfortCleansing & Preparation — Skin is thoroughly cleansed and prepared. A sterile, single-use cartridge is loaded into the Dermapen®Microneedling Treatment — The Dermapen® is passed over the skin in methodical passes, creating controlled micro-channels. Therapeutic serums (hyaluronic acid, growth factors, vitamin C) are applied simultaneously for deep infusionPost-Treatment Application — Soothing serum and SPF are applied. Aftercare instructions are provided in writingRecovery & Results — Skin appears red (similar to a mild sunburn) for 24–48 hours, then settles. New collagen formation continues over 4–6 weeks
expectedResults: Days 1–3: Skin appears pink/red and may feel tight — this is the active healing phase. Week 1–2: Redness resolves; skin may feel smoother early. Weeks 3–4: New collagen formation begins to improve texture and pore size. Weeks 4–8: Significant improvement in skin quality, scarring, and overall radiance visible. Weeks 8–12: Full results of the treatment cycle are visible — typically requiring 3–6 sessions total.

faqs.question: Is Dermapen microneedling safe for darker skin tones?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is one of the key advantages of Dermapen over ablative laser for South African patients. Microneedling does not use heat or light energy, meaning it does not carry the pigmentation risk (post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation or hypopigmentation) that some laser treatments can cause in Fitzpatrick IV–VI skin tones. It is safe and effective for all skin tones.

faqs.question: How many Dermapen sessions do I need?
faqs.answer: For general skin rejuvenation and anti-ageing, 3 sessions (4–6 weeks apart) deliver excellent results. For acne scarring, 4–6 sessions are typically needed for significant improvement. Severe scarring may require more. Dr. Bangalee will give you a realistic expectation at your first consultation.

faqs.question: Does microneedling hurt?
faqs.answer: Topical anaesthetic cream is applied 30–45 minutes before treatment, making Dermapen very well-tolerated. Most patients describe a mild vibrating or scratching sensation rather than pain. The face may feel flushed and warm immediately after — this settles within hours.

faqs.question: How long does Dermapen last?
faqs.answer: The new collagen stimulated by Dermapen continues to remodel for up to 6 months post-treatment. Results from a full series of 3–6 treatments can last 12–18 months, depending on skin type, age, and lifestyle. Quarterly maintenance treatments are recommended for ongoing rejuvenation.

faqs.question: Can Dermapen help with acne scars?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is one of Dermapen's most impactful applications. Microneedling breaks down the fibrous scar tissue that creates textural irregularities and stimulates the formation of new, smoother collagen. Multiple sessions are required for significant scar improvement — typically 4–6 sessions for moderate to severe acne scarring.

faqs.question: What serums are used during Dermapen treatment?
faqs.answer: Dr. Bangalee selects serums based on your specific concern: hyaluronic acid for hydration and plumping, vitamin C for brightening and antioxidant support, growth factor serums for enhanced healing and collagen stimulation, or platelet-rich plasma (PRP) for advanced skin regeneration. The micro-channels created by Dermapen dramatically increase serum absorption.

faqs.question: When can I wear makeup after microneedling?
faqs.answer: Avoid makeup for the first 24–48 hours to allow the micro-channels to close and prevent the risk of infection. From day 3, mineral powder foundation is appropriate. Full makeup can typically resume from day 3–5 depending on how quickly your skin heals.

faqs.question: Can I get Dermapen done if I have active acne?
faqs.answer: Active inflamed acne is a contraindication for microneedling over the affected areas — the risk of spreading bacteria and worsening infection is significant. We recommend managing active acne first, then using Dermapen to address the residual scarring. Dr. Bangalee will assess and advise appropriately.

faqs.question: Is Dermapen better than a home derma-roller?
faqs.answer: Dramatically so. Dermapen uses medical-grade, sterile, single-use cartridges with precisely controlled needle depth (0.1mm–2.5mm). Home rollers use dull, fixed-depth needles that create tearing rather than clean micro-channels, carry significant infection risk, and cannot achieve the depths required for collagen stimulation. Never use a home roller on active acne scarring — it can worsen scarring.

faqs.question: Can Dermapen tighten loose skin?
faqs.answer: Yes. The collagen stimulation from microneedling improves skin elasticity and can provide a meaningful tightening effect — particularly in areas of mild laxity like the lower face, neck, and jowls. For more significant skin laxity, Dr. Bangalee may recommend combining Dermapen with other treatments.

title: Excessive Sweating Treatment (Hyperhidrosis)
tagline: Stop Excessive Sweating — Regain Your Confidence
quickSummary: Botulinum Toxin (Botox®) injected into the underarms, palms, or soles of the feet temporarily blocks the nerve signals to sweat glands, delivering dramatic, long-lasting relief from excessive sweating — with no surgery, no downtime, and no side effects.
downtimeDetail: Zero. You can return to work, dress normally, and resume all activities immediately. Avoid strenuous exercise and hot baths for 24 hours.
suitableFor: Anyone whose sweating is excessive, unpredictable, or socially/professionally debilitatingThose who have tried prescription strength antiperspirants and found them inadequatePeople who avoid certain clothing colours, materials, or social situations because of sweatingThose with sweaty palms that affect professional handshakes, writing, or using devicesPatients who have been diagnosed with hyperhidrosis and are seeking medical treatment
heroText: Excessive sweating (hyperhidrosis) is a medical condition — not a hygiene problem. If you avoid social situations, wear only dark clothing, or feel defined by your sweating, there is an effective medical solution available at Star Aesthetic Medical Centre that delivers 6–9 months of relief from a single treatment.
whatIs: Hyperhidrosis is a medical condition characterised by excessive sweating that goes beyond normal thermoregulatory sweating. It can be primary focal hyperhidrosis (affecting specific areas — most commonly underarms, hands, and feet, with no underlying medical cause) or secondary generalised hyperhidrosis (caused by an underlying medical condition or medication).At Star Aesthetic, we treat primary focal hyperhidrosis using Botulinum Toxin (Botox®) — the most effective, evidence-based medical treatment for this condition. The toxin is injected into the skin of the affected area and blocks the neurotransmitter signal (acetylcholine) to the sweat glands, temporarily but dramatically reducing sweat production. Results last 6–9 months from a single treatment session.
howWorks: Consultation — Dr. Bangalee assesses your sweating pattern, conducts a starch-iodine test (to map exactly where sweating is heaviest), reviews medical history, and confirms hyperhidrosis diagnosisMapping the Treatment Zone — The starch-iodine test identifies the exact areas of maximum sweating activity, which are mapped with a skin marker to guide precise injectionsTopical Anaesthetic (Optional) — Anaesthetic cream can be applied to the underarm area if requested. For hands and feet, a nerve block is offeredInjections — Multiple small injections of Botulinum Toxin are administered at regular intervals across the mapped area using a very fine needlePost-Treatment — You can dress and return to normal activities immediately. Avoid hot baths and strenuous exercise for 24 hours
expectedResults: Days 5–7: Initial reduction in sweating becomes noticeable. Day 14: Full effect — sweating in treated areas is dramatically reduced. Duration: 6–9 months for most patients (longer than standard cosmetic Botox due to reduced muscle activity in the area). Repeat treatment is straightforward and maintains the same level of relief.

faqs.question: How do I know if my sweating is medically excessive?
faqs.answer: If your sweating occurs regardless of temperature or exercise, is unpredictable, lasts more than 6 months, affects both sides of the body equally, and interferes with your daily life or emotional wellbeing — these are the diagnostic criteria for primary hyperhidrosis. Dr. Bangalee will conduct a clinical assessment to confirm the diagnosis.

faqs.question: How long does Botox for sweating last?
faqs.answer: For hyperhidrosis, Botox typically lasts 6–9 months — significantly longer than the 3–5 months for cosmetic facial Botox. This is because the sweat glands of the underarm require higher doses for longer activity, and there is no dynamic muscle activity to break down the toxin faster.

faqs.question: Will Botox for sweating affect my health — do I need to sweat to regulate temperature?
faqs.answer: No. The body has between 2–4 million sweat glands — treating a small area of underarms, hands, or feet blocks only a tiny fraction. The body's overall thermoregulatory capacity is completely unaffected. You will still sweat normally everywhere else.

faqs.question: Is the treatment painful?
faqs.answer: Underarm injections are well-tolerated with mild stinging at each injection point. Hands and feet are more sensitive areas — we offer a nerve block for palmar treatment. Most patients say the procedure is much more comfortable than anticipated.

faqs.question: Can excessive sweating come back after Botox?
faqs.answer: Yes — the treatment is temporary, lasting 6–9 months. As the Botox gradually wears off, sweating returns to its previous level. Repeat treatment at this point restores the same degree of relief. There is no evidence that Botox becomes less effective with repeated treatments over time.

faqs.question: Will I have compensatory sweating in other areas after treatment?
faqs.answer: A small percentage of patients experience a mild increase in sweating in adjacent areas — this is more common with very extensive treatment. Dr. Bangalee manages this risk through careful dosing.

faqs.question: Does medical aid cover hyperhidrosis Botox?
faqs.answer: Some medical aids provide benefit for clinically diagnosed hyperhidrosis (ICD-10 code R61). We recommend contacting your medical aid scheme directly and requesting pre-authorisation. We can provide the necessary clinical documentation to support your claim.

faqs.question: Can I use deodorant after the treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes — as usual. The Botox does not interact with antiperspirant or deodorant. Many patients choose to use a lighter or more natural deodorant once treatment is effective, as heavy antiperspirant is no longer necessary.

faqs.question: How quickly can I book for excessive sweating treatment?
faqs.answer: Contact us at +27 (0)31 573 1325 or info@staraesthetic.site. We will book a consultation and typically treat in the same appointment if you are medically suitable. Book as soon as possible before important events — allow 2 weeks for the treatment to reach full effect.

faqs.question: Is this the same Botox used for wrinkles?
faqs.answer: Yes — the same Botulinum Toxin Type A used for facial wrinkle relaxation. The difference is the injection technique and the anatomical target: for hyperhidrosis, small doses are injected intradermally (into the skin) at closely spaced intervals across the sweat-producing area, rather than deeper into muscles.

title: Jaw & Chin Contouring
tagline: Define Your Profile — Non-Surgical Jawline & Chin Enhancement in Durban
quickSummary: Jaw and chin contouring uses dermal fillers to add definition and projection to an underdeveloped chin or jawline, and Botox to slim an over-developed masseter muscle (square jaw) — creating a more balanced, harmonious facial profile.
priceFrom: R 2,500 – R 8,000 (depending on areas and product volume)
downtimeDetail: 24–48 hours mild swelling and possible bruising (cover with makeup from day 2). Avoid strenuous exercise, excessive heat, and pressure on treated areas for 48 hours.
suitableFor: Women wanting a more defined, sculpted jawline and feminine facial ovalMen desiring stronger jaw definition and chin projectionThose whose chin is receding or weak in profile, making the nose appear prominentWomen with a heavy or square jaw (masseter hypertrophy) wanting a more tapered, oval face shapeAnyone wanting lower face enhancement without surgical implants
heroText: A well-defined jawline and balanced chin are among the most requested aesthetic improvements by both women and men. At Star Aesthetic Medical Centre, Dr. Bangalee offers a combination of dermal fillers and Botox to sculpt, define, and balance the lower face — delivering profile transformation without surgery.
whatIs: Jaw and chin contouring is the art of using injectable treatments to improve the balance, definition, and proportion of the lower face. It encompasses three complementary treatments: chin augmentation (adding projection to a receding chin using dermal filler), jawline definition (building a stronger, more defined jaw contour using filler along the mandibular line), and jaw slimming (reducing masseter muscle bulk in patients with a square or heavy jaw using Botox).The lower face and profile are among the most powerful determinants of overall facial harmony. A weak chin makes the nose appear larger and the neck less defined. A square, heavy jaw can create a masculine appearance in women. The right combination of filler and Botox can dramatically improve facial proportions without any surgery.
howWorks: Facial Analysis & Consultation — Dr. Bangalee conducts a detailed facial proportions assessment using the Phi ratio and standardised aesthetic measurements. Goals are discussed and a treatment plan designedPhotographs — Before photos are taken from multiple angles for comparisonPreparation — Treatment area is cleansed. For filler: topical anaesthetic or dental block as required. For Botox: no anaesthetic neededChin Filler — Precisely placed dermal filler adds projection, height, or width to the chin depending on the individual assessmentJawline Filler — Filler is placed along the mandibular line to create definition and sharpnessMasseter Botox — For jaw slimming, Botox is injected bilaterally into the masseter muscles. Full slimming effect develops over 4–6 weeksReview — A 2–4 week review allows fine-tuning of results
expectedResults: Immediate: Filler results are visible from treatment, though mild swelling may temporarily obscure the final result. 24–48 hours: Swelling resolves; filler result clearly visible. 4–6 weeks: Masseter Botox slimming effect becomes apparent. 3 months: Full combined result is visible.

faqs.question: Is non-surgical chin augmentation as effective as a chin implant?
faqs.answer: For mild to moderate chin projection needs, dermal filler delivers excellent, natural-looking results without surgery. Chin implants provide permanent augmentation and are more appropriate for larger projection needs. Dr. Bangalee will advise which is appropriate for your goals and anatomy — most patients achieve excellent results with filler.

faqs.question: How long does jawline filler last?
faqs.answer: The jawline and chin are low-movement areas — filler placed here tends to last 12–18 months or longer. Individual results vary based on the product used, your metabolism, and lifestyle factors.

faqs.question: Will jaw slimming Botox change how I eat or speak?
faqs.answer: No — masseter Botox does not affect normal eating, speaking, or jaw function. The masseter is the primary chewing muscle, and the doses used for slimming reduce its bulk without affecting its function. Some patients notice mild jaw fatigue when chewing very tough foods in the first few weeks — this resolves as the muscle adapts.

faqs.question: Can jaw and chin contouring make my nose look smaller?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is one of the most powerful yet least discussed effects of chin augmentation. A receding chin makes the nose appear more prominent by comparison. Projecting the chin forward and strengthening the jawline creates a more balanced facial profile that makes the nose appear more proportionate — without any nasal surgery.

faqs.question: Is there a difference between jaw contouring for men and women?
faqs.answer: Yes — the aesthetic goals are different. For women, we typically aim to create a more tapered, oval lower face with subtle definition. For men, we aim for stronger, more angular definition. The techniques are similar but the injection points, volumes, and product selection differ to maintain gender-appropriate aesthetics.

faqs.question: Can jaw and chin filler be dissolved if I don't like the result?
faqs.answer: Yes — hyaluronic acid fillers can be dissolved with hyaluronidase (Hyalase) enzyme at any time. This provides complete peace of mind for patients trying filler for the first time.

faqs.question: What is the difference between jaw fillers and jawline Botox?
faqs.answer: Jawline filler adds volume and definition along the jaw bone — creating shape and structure. Masseter Botox reduces the bulk of the chewing muscle for a slimmer jaw shape. These address different aspects of jaw aesthetics — Dr. Bangalee often combines both in the same session for comprehensive contouring.

faqs.question: How long does jaw slimming Botox take to work?
faqs.answer: Masseter Botox takes 4–6 weeks to show slimming results as the muscle gradually weakens and shrinks from reduced use. The full slimming effect can continue to develop for up to 3 months. It lasts 4–6 months initially, with longer duration after repeated treatments.

faqs.question: Are there risks with jaw and chin fillers?
faqs.answer: When performed by an experienced aesthetic physician like Dr. Bangalee, jaw and chin filler is safe. Risks include temporary bruising, swelling, and asymmetry — all correctable. The chin and jaw region contains arteries that must be avoided — this is why treatment must only be performed by a medically qualified and anatomically trained injector.

faqs.question: Can I combine jaw contouring with Botox for wrinkles in the same session?
faqs.answer: Absolutely — this is very common. Many patients address both wrinkle relaxation (forehead, frown, crow's feet) and lower face contouring in a single efficient appointment. Dr. Bangalee creates a comprehensive facial aesthetic plan for patients wanting multi-area treatment.

title: Lip Filler Treatment
tagline: Natural-Looking Lip Enhancement — Volume, Definition & Balance
quickSummary: Lip fillers use hyaluronic acid (a naturally occurring sugar molecule) to add volume, improve definition, and restore or enhance the natural shape and proportion of the lips — safely, reversibly, and with results lasting 6–12 months.
priceFrom: R 2,500 – R 4,500 (depending on amount of filler)
downtimeDetail: 24–48 hours mild-to-moderate lip swelling (can be covered with lip gloss). Bruising is possible but not universal. Avoid intense exercise, heat exposure, and alcohol for 24 hours. Avoid dental procedures for 2 weeks.
suitableFor: Women wanting natural lip enhancement — more volume, better definition, or improved symmetryThose whose lips have thinned with age and who want to restore a more youthful lip appearanceWomen with a naturally thin upper lip wanting subtle volumeAnyone wanting to refine the shape of their lip border, enhance the cupid's bow, or address lip asymmetryPatients wanting to understand their options before committing — Dr. Bangalee is happy to start conservatively
heroText: Fuller, more defined lips are one of the most requested aesthetic treatments worldwide — and when done with the right technique and the right amount of filler, the result is beautiful, natural, and unmistakably you. At Star Aesthetic, Dr. Bangalee's philosophy for lip filler is always subtlety first — enhancing what you have rather than creating something that doesn't belong on your face.
whatIs: Lip fillers use hyaluronic acid (HA) — a sugar molecule naturally produced in the body — to add volume, shape, definition, and hydration to the lips. HA filler is injected in small, precise amounts at specific anatomical points to achieve the patient's desired result — whether that is subtle enhancement, proportional balancing, or more significant volume.Hyaluronic acid is reversible (it can be dissolved with hyaluronidase enzyme at any time), gradually breaks down naturally over 6–12 months, and integrates very naturally into lip tissue. It also attracts and binds water — keeping the lips hydrated and supple.Dr. Bangalee's approach to lip filler is always proportion-based — assessing the natural relationship between upper and lower lip, the lip-to-face ratio, and the individual's facial features to create a result that enhances rather than dominates.
howWorks: Consultation — Goals are discussed and Dr. Bangalee assesses your lip anatomy, facial proportions, and natural lip shape to create a personalised filler planPhotographs — Multiple angle photos are taken before treatmentTopical Anaesthetic — EMLA cream is applied to the lip area for 20–30 minutes. Most modern lip fillers also contain lidocaine for additional comfort during injectionInjection — Using a fine needle or blunt cannula, hyaluronic acid filler is placed precisely at the anatomical points required for your desired result. Injection is slow and controlled for accuracyMassage & Shaping — Dr. Bangalee gently shapes the filler immediately after injection to ensure even distribution and the desired contourIce & Recovery — Ice is applied to minimise swelling. You will receive written aftercare instructions
expectedResults: Immediately: Lips appear swollen and possibly asymmetrical — this is normal. 24–48 hours: Swelling begins to settle. 72 hours: Most swelling resolved; result becoming visible. 2 weeks: Final result — all swelling completely resolved. Duration: 6–12 months (varies by individual metabolism, amount of filler, and whether you move your lips frequently through speaking and eating).

faqs.question: Will lip fillers look natural?
faqs.answer: Natural-looking results depend entirely on the injector's aesthetic judgment, technique, and the amount of filler used. Dr. Bangalee's philosophy is always proportion-appropriate results — he will never recommend more filler than your facial anatomy can naturally support. If you are concerned, start with 0.5ml — this is almost always undetectable to anyone who doesn't know your lips before.

faqs.question: Do lip fillers hurt?
faqs.answer: Topical anaesthetic cream is applied for 20–30 minutes before treatment, and modern lip fillers contain lidocaine (anaesthetic) in the syringe. Most patients rate lip filler discomfort as 3–4 out of 10 — very tolerable. The sensitivity of lips varies significantly between patients.

faqs.question: How long do lip fillers last?
faqs.answer: Typically 6–12 months. Lips are a very mobile area — all the movement from speaking, eating, smiling, and kissing metabolises filler faster than less active areas. Smaller amounts (0.5ml) may last 6 months; larger volumes (1–2ml) often last 9–12 months.

faqs.question: Can lip fillers be reversed?
faqs.answer: Yes — hyaluronic acid filler can be completely dissolved using hyaluronidase (Hyalase) enzyme. Results reverse within 24–48 hours of the dissolving treatment. This is one of the major advantages of HA filler over permanent lip augmentation methods.

faqs.question: What is the difference between lip filler and a lip flip?
faqs.answer: Lip filler adds volume using hyaluronic acid. A lip flip uses a very small amount of Botox injected into the orbicularis oris muscle around the upper lip, causing it to slightly evert (flip outward) — giving the illusion of a fuller upper lip border without actually adding volume. A lip flip lasts 6–8 weeks and costs significantly less (from R 800) — it is a good option for patients wanting subtle upper lip definition without committing to filler.

faqs.question: Can I get lip fillers if I have thin lips naturally?
faqs.answer: Yes — thin lips are one of the most common indications for lip filler. Natural anatomy is always respected — Dr. Bangalee will not create a result that looks disproportionate to your facial structure. For naturally thin lips, we recommend starting with 0.5–1ml and building gradually over 1–2 sessions.

faqs.question: What should I avoid before my lip filler appointment?
faqs.answer: Avoid blood-thinning medications and supplements (aspirin, ibuprofen, fish oil, vitamin E, alcohol) for 48–72 hours before treatment to minimise bruising risk. If you have a history of cold sores (HSV-1), inform Dr. Bangalee — you may need antiviral prophylaxis before lip filler treatment.

faqs.question: I am worried about getting 'duck lips.' How do I avoid this?
faqs.answer: Duck lips (disproportionate, overfilled lips) result from too much filler, poor placement, or inappropriate product selection. Dr. Bangalee's anatomy-based approach and conservative philosophy eliminates this risk. The key is working with an experienced aesthetic physician who prioritises your facial harmony over simply filling. Show photos of results you like — communication is everything.

faqs.question: Can lip fillers help with vertical lip lines?
faqs.answer: Yes and no. Lip filler adds volume that can push out fine perioral (around the mouth) lines from within. For deeper, more established lip lines, a combination of lip filler for volume and small amounts of Botox for the vertical lines delivers the best result. Dr. Bangalee frequently combines these in the same session.

faqs.question: How old do I need to be to get lip fillers in South Africa?
faqs.answer: You must be 18 or older for cosmetic injectable treatments in South Africa. Dr. Bangalee does not treat patients under 18. We request valid ID for all first-time patients.

title: Medi-Lean Medical Weight Loss Programme
tagline: Medically Supervised Weight Management — Real Results, Sustained Long-Term
quickSummary: The Medi-Lean Weight Loss Programme combines FDA-approved prescription medication (including GLP-1 receptor agonists), personalised nutritional guidance, and regular physician monitoring to deliver meaningful, sustainable weight loss under full medical supervision.
downtimeDetail: None. The programme integrates fully into your normal life.
suitableFor: Adults with a BMI ≥ 27 and at least one weight-related health condition (hypertension, type 2 diabetes, dyslipidaemia)Adults with a BMI ≥ 30 regardless of comorbiditiesThose who have tried diet and exercise consistently and struggled to maintain meaningful weight lossPatients wanting medically supervised, evidence-based weight management rather than fad dietsThose wanting to combine weight loss with aesthetic body contouring treatments for comprehensive results
heroText: Weight management is one of the most complex challenges in medicine — and one of the most personally significant. At Star Aesthetic Medical Centre, the Medi-Lean programme provides medically supervised weight loss combining prescription medication, nutritional guidance, and regular clinical monitoring by Dr. Bangalee. This is not a diet — it is a comprehensive medical weight management solution.
whatIs: The Medi-Lean Weight Loss Programme is Star Aesthetic's medical approach to weight management — going far beyond 'eat less, move more' to address the clinical, hormonal, and psychological complexity of obesity and overweight.The programme utilises the latest generation of prescription weight loss medications — including GLP-1 receptor agonists (semaglutide, liraglutide, tirzepatide) — which have revolutionised the medical management of obesity by addressing the hormonal dysregulation that drives appetite, satiety, and fat storage. These medications are combined with personalised nutritional coaching, body composition monitoring, and regular physician-supervised adjustment.Medi-Lean is appropriate for patients with a BMI of 27 or above with weight-related health concerns, or BMI of 30 and above. It is a clinical programme — not a beauty treatment — and Dr. Bangalee conducts a thorough medical assessment before prescribing any medication.
howWorks: Initial Medical Assessment — Comprehensive review of medical history, current medications, BMI, body composition, and blood tests (HbA1c, lipids, thyroid function, etc.)Personalised Programme Design — Dr. Bangalee designs your personalised protocol: medication selection, starting dose, nutritional targets, and activity recommendationsPrescription Medication — Where clinically appropriate, prescription weight management medication is prescribed (GLP-1 receptor agonist or alternative)Nutritional Guidance — Detailed dietary guidance is provided — not a restrictive diet, but a sustainable caloric and macro approach that works with the medicationMonthly Monitoring Consultations — Weight, body measurements, blood pressure, and medication tolerance are monitored at monthly appointmentsProgramme Adjustment — Medication dose is titrated according to response and tolerability; nutritional guidance is adjusted as the programme progresses
expectedResults: Month 1–2: Initial weight loss of 1–4kg as medication takes effect and dietary changes are adopted. Month 3–4: Increasing momentum — 0.5–1.5kg per week in the effective range. Month 6: Many patients achieve 8–15% body weight reduction at this point. Month 12: Clinical trials show 15–20%+ body weight reduction with GLP-1 medications at optimal doses in compliant patients.

faqs.question: What is a GLP-1 receptor agonist and how does it cause weight loss?
faqs.answer: GLP-1 (Glucagon-Like Peptide 1) is a hormone naturally produced in the gut after eating — it signals the brain to reduce appetite, slows gastric emptying (keeping you feeling full longer), and improves insulin sensitivity. GLP-1 receptor agonists (medications like semaglutide/Ozempic® and tirzepatide/Mounjaro®) mimic this hormone at much higher and sustained concentrations, producing significant and consistent appetite reduction and metabolic benefits.

faqs.question: Is Ozempic available in South Africa for weight loss?
faqs.answer: Semaglutide (the active ingredient in Ozempic® and Wegovy®) is available in South Africa. Supply has been constrained due to global demand — Dr. Bangalee monitors availability and will advise on the currently available formulation at consultation. Alternative GLP-1 medications (including liraglutide and tirzepatide) may be available as alternatives.

faqs.question: How much weight can I expect to lose?
faqs.answer: Clinical trials with GLP-1 receptor agonists show average weight loss of 10–20% of body weight over 12 months for compliant patients. Results vary significantly by individual, starting weight, metabolic profile, dietary adherence, and activity levels. Dr. Bangalee will give you a realistic expectation based on your starting point.

faqs.question: Is the medication safe?
faqs.answer: GLP-1 receptor agonists are rigorously studied and approved by multiple regulatory bodies including the FDA. They are among the most studied weight management medications in history. Dr. Bangalee conducts a thorough medical assessment to confirm suitability and contraindications before any prescription. Regular monitoring ensures safety throughout the programme.

faqs.question: Will I regain the weight when I stop the medication?
faqs.answer: There is evidence that weight regain can occur after stopping GLP-1 medications, as the hormonal drivers of appetite return to baseline. This is why we combine medication with lifestyle and nutritional education — to build habits that support weight maintenance independently. Many patients continue at a lower maintenance dose long-term.

faqs.question: Can I use the weight loss programme alongside body contouring?
faqs.answer: Yes — and this is often the optimal approach. Significant weight loss followed by body contouring for residual fat deposits and skin tightening delivers comprehensive results. Dr. Bangalee can advise the best sequencing of medical weight loss and aesthetic body treatments.

faqs.question: Do I need blood tests before starting the programme?
faqs.answer: Yes — a baseline blood panel is required before initiating prescription medication. This includes HbA1c (blood sugar), lipid panel, thyroid function, kidney function, and a full blood count. This ensures safety and also provides a baseline for measuring metabolic improvements as weight loss progresses.

faqs.question: Can the Medi-Lean programme help with Type 2 diabetes?
faqs.answer: GLP-1 receptor agonists were originally developed for Type 2 diabetes management — weight loss is an additional significant benefit. If you have Type 2 diabetes, these medications may simultaneously improve your blood glucose control and support weight loss. This requires careful coordination with your treating physician or endocrinologist.

faqs.question: What dietary guidance is included in the programme?
faqs.answer: Dr. Bangalee provides a personalised nutritional framework — not a restrictive crash diet, but a structured approach to caloric intake, macronutrient balance, and meal timing that works synergistically with the medication. The focus is on sustainable eating patterns, not short-term food restriction.

faqs.question: Is this programme appropriate for post-menopausal weight gain?
faqs.answer: Yes. Menopausal weight gain is driven by hormonal changes that alter fat distribution and metabolic rate — and it is one of the most frustrating and common clinical presentations in an aesthetic medical practice. GLP-1 medications work independently of oestrogen levels and are effective for post-menopausal weight management.

title: Pigmentation Treatment
tagline: Clinically Proven Solutions for Dark Spots, Melasma & Uneven Skin Tone
quickSummary: Pigmentation treatment at Star Aesthetic combines professional in-clinic treatments (cosmelan®, chemical peels, Dermapen) with a medically supervised home-care regimen (melan tran3x, SPF) to address the full spectrum of hyperpigmentation — from mild sun spots to stubborn melasma.
downtime: Cosmelan: 5–7 days peeling/sensitivity; Peels: 2–5 days; Dermapen: 24–48 hours
downtimeDetail: cosmelan® mask: 5–7 days of peeling, redness, and sensitivity. Chemical peels: 2–5 days of mild peeling and sensitivity. Dermapen: 24–48 hours redness. Home-care products: occasional mild sensitivity during adaptation phase.
suitableFor: Women with melasma — hormonally-driven pigmentation often worsening with the pill, pregnancy, or menopauseThose with post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation from acne, trauma, or previous treatmentsAnyone with sun-induced dark spots and uneven skin tone — particularly relevant in KZN's high-UV coastal environmentAll Fitzpatrick skin types — our protocols are adapted for South African skin diversityThose who have tried over-the-counter brightening products without meaningful results
heroText: Uneven skin tone, dark spots, and melasma are among the most common and most frustrating aesthetic concerns for South African women. In Durban's high-UV coastal climate, pigmentation is a daily battle. At Star Aesthetic Medical Centre, Dr. Bangalee offers clinically proven in-clinic and home-care protocols that deliver real, lasting depigmentation results.
whatIs: Hyperpigmentation is the result of excess melanin production in localised areas of the skin. It can be caused by UV exposure (solar lentigines/sun spots), hormonal fluctuations (melasma — often called the 'mask of pregnancy'), post-inflammatory triggers (post-acne dark marks, PIH), or a combination of all three.In South Africa — with its high UV index year-round and population with a range of Fitzpatrick skin types III–VI (all of which are prone to melanin excess) — pigmentation is the single most common aesthetic concern in aesthetic practice.At Star Aesthetic, pigmentation treatment is approached as a programme rather than a single treatment — because the most effective results come from combining professional in-clinic depigmentation with a rigorously maintained home-care protocol. Treating pigmentation without daily SPF application is futile — UV exposure rebuilds dark spots as fast as treatment removes them.Our treatments include the globally recognised cosmelan® depigmentation system (mesoestetic), professional chemical peels, Dermapen microneedling with brightening serums, and a medically supervised home-care programme using melan tran3x and mesoprotech® melan 130.
howWorks: Comprehensive Pigmentation Assessment — Dr. Bangalee identifies the type and depth of your pigmentation using Wood's lamp examination where appropriate, assesses Fitzpatrick skin type, and designs a personalised treatment programmeHome-Care Programme Initiation — melan tran3x Concentrate and mesoprotech® melan 130 SPF are prescribed to begin the depigmentation process and establish daily SPF compliance before in-clinic treatmentIn-Clinic Treatment Sessions — Depending on pigmentation type and severity: cosmelan® intensive depigmentation mask (single in-clinic application), professional chemical peels (mesoestetic mesopeel), or Dermapen with brightening serumsPost-Treatment Support — Full aftercare protocol is provided. SPF compliance is the non-negotiable element of every pigmentation programmeProgress Monitoring — Photograph comparison at each visit tracks measurable improvementMaintenance Programme — Ongoing skin-tone maintenance through continued use of depigmenting skincare and periodic in-clinic treatments
expectedResults: Week 1–2: Home-care depigmenting actives begin working; cosmelan® peeling phase if applicable. Week 3–4: Early fading of surface pigmentation visible; skin tone becoming more even. Week 6–8: Significant reduction in dark spot intensity; improved overall skin radiance. Week 10–12: Substantial pigmentation correction; continued improvement with ongoing home-care.

faqs.question: What is the best treatment for melasma in Durban?
faqs.answer: There is no single 'best' — the most effective approach to melasma is a combination programme: cosmelan® (or professional peels) for intensive in-clinic depigmentation, melan tran3x Concentrate for daily home-care treatment, and mesoprotech® melan 130 SPF50+ for year-round UV protection. Treating any one element in isolation produces partial results. Book a consultation with Dr. Bangalee for your personalised programme.

faqs.question: Why does my pigmentation keep coming back?
faqs.answer: Pigmentation — especially melasma — is a chronic condition, not a one-time problem. The melanocytes (pigment-producing cells) that were previously overactive remain primed to re-respond to UV exposure and hormonal triggers. Without consistent daily SPF and an ongoing home-care protocol, pigmentation recurs. This is not treatment failure — it is the nature of the condition.

faqs.question: Is it safe to treat pigmentation on darker skin tones?
faqs.answer: Yes — with the right protocols. Certain treatments (particularly aggressive lasers or high-strength peels) can cause post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (paradoxical darkening) in Fitzpatrick IV–VI skin. At Star Aesthetic, we use protocols specifically appropriate for South African skin diversity — including the melan tran3x system and mesoestetic mesopeel — all formulated and clinically tested for darker skin types.

faqs.question: What is cosmelan® and how is it different from a regular peel?
faqs.answer: cosmelan® is mesoestetic's globally recognised intensive depigmentation system — the most widely used professional depigmentation treatment in the world. Unlike standard chemical peels that primarily exfoliate, cosmelan® acts specifically on the melanin production pathway through multiple mechanisms simultaneously, delivering significantly more targeted and intensive depigmentation. It is applied in-clinic as a single treatment followed by a specific home-care maintenance protocol.

faqs.question: Do I really need to wear sunscreen every day?
faqs.answer: Yes — non-negotiably. UV exposure is the primary driver of pigmentation, and sunscreen is the most powerful depigmenting tool available. Without daily SPF50+, every in-clinic treatment and home-care product is partially undone by daily sun exposure. In Durban's UV environment, this is especially critical year-round.

faqs.question: How long does pigmentation treatment take?
faqs.answer: Meaningful results require a minimum of 8–12 weeks of consistent treatment. Full results typically develop over 3–6 months of a complete programme. Pigmentation treatment is a commitment, not a quick fix — but the results of a properly followed programme are transformative and lasting when maintained.

faqs.question: Can the pill or HRT cause pigmentation?
faqs.answer: Yes. Hormonal contraceptives (the pill, hormonal IUDs) and hormone replacement therapy (HRT) are known triggers for melasma in susceptible women. The combination of exogenous oestrogen and UV exposure activates melanocytes in hormonally sensitive individuals. Changing contraceptive method may be part of the management approach — discuss this with both Dr. Bangalee and your gynaecologist.

faqs.question: Can I treat pigmentation during pregnancy?
faqs.answer: Pregnancy itself is a common cause of melasma ('mask of pregnancy'). Treatment options during pregnancy are restricted — many brightening actives (retinoids, AHAs, hydroquinone) are contraindicated. Safe options are limited to gentle non-active skincare and strict SPF use. Dr. Bangalee will advise the safest approach for pregnant patients.

faqs.question: Will skin peels help with my dark spots?
faqs.answer: Yes — professional chemical peels (salicylic, glycolic, mandelic, or mesoestetic mesopeel) deliver controlled exfoliation that accelerates cell turnover and removes pigmented surface cells. Multiple peel sessions combined with home-care produce meaningful lightening of superficial dark spots. Deeper pigmentation (melasma reaching the dermis) requires more intensive treatment.

faqs.question: Is there a permanent solution to pigmentation?
faqs.answer: Superficial sun spots can be substantially cleared with the right treatment programme. Melasma, however, is a chronic condition — the tendency to produce excess melanin in response to UV and hormonal triggers is lifelong. With the right ongoing programme (SPF + home-care + periodic in-clinic maintenance), melasma can be very effectively suppressed, but it requires consistent management.

title: Skin Peel Treatment (Chemical Peel)
tagline: Professional Skin Resurfacing — Reveal Brighter, Smoother, Younger Skin
quickSummary: Chemical peels use pharmaceutical-grade acids to precisely exfoliate the skin's surface and upper layers, stimulating cell renewal, collagen production, and the emergence of fresher, brighter skin. Treatments range from gentle lunch-hour peels to medium-depth resurfacing.
downtimeDetail: Superficial peels: 2–4 days of mild flaking and sensitivity. Medium-depth peels: 5–7 days of visible peeling and redness — social downtime advised. All peel types: strict SPF use for minimum 4 weeks post-peel, and no direct sun exposure.
suitableFor: Women and men wanting to improve overall skin texture, radiance, and toneThose with sun damage, fine lines, or dull, lifeless skinPatients with oily, acne-prone skin wanting deep pore cleansing and sebum controlAnyone with hyperpigmentation, dark spots, or melasma (as part of a complete depigmentation programme)Those wanting an effective, professional skin treatment without significant downtime
heroText: A professional skin peel at Star Aesthetic Medical Centre is one of the most effective and versatile treatments available for improving skin texture, clarity, pigmentation, and overall skin quality — in one of the most affordable treatment categories in aesthetic medicine. Dr. Bangalee selects from mesoestetic's professional mesopeel® range to deliver precisely calibrated results for every skin type and concern.
whatIs: A chemical peel is a controlled application of an acidic solution to the skin surface, causing exfoliation of the outer skin layers and stimulating new cell growth, collagen production, and overall skin renewal. The depth of the peel — superficial, medium, or deep — is determined by the type and concentration of acid used, and is precisely calibrated to the patient's skin concern, type, and tolerance.At Star Aesthetic, we use the mesoestetic mesopeel® professional peel range — widely considered the gold standard in professional chemical peels. mesoestetic was founded by the developers of the first professional glycolic acid peel, and their mesopeel® range encompasses specific formulations for pigmentation, acne, anti-ageing, and sensitive skin.Dr. Bangalee selects the specific peel type and concentration based on your individual skin assessment, ensuring the most effective and safest outcome for your skin type and concern.
howWorks: Consultation & Skin Assessment — Dr. Bangalee assesses your skin type, Fitzpatrick classification, concerns, and any previous peel experience to select the most appropriate peel formulationPre-Peel Preparation — For deeper peels, 2–4 weeks of home-care priming (retinol, AHA) may be recommended to improve results and reduce complicationsSkin Cleansing & Degreasing — The skin is thoroughly cleansed and degreased with a pre-peel solution to ensure even penetrationPeel Application — The selected peel solution is applied in controlled layers. You will feel warmth, tingling, or mild stinging — all expectedNeutralisation (where applicable) — Some peel formulations require neutralisation with a bicarbonate solution; others self-neutralisePost-Peel Treatment — Soothing serum, moisturiser, and SPF50+ are applied. Written aftercare instructions are provided
expectedResults: Day 1: Skin appears flushed; you may feel a mild 'sunburn' sensation. Days 2–4: Peeling begins — skin sheds (this is the new skin emerging). Days 5–7: Peeling complete; new, brighter skin visible. Week 2 onwards: Skin continues to improve as collagen remodelling and cell renewal progress. Peak results at 2–4 weeks post-peel, and cumulative improvement with each subsequent session.

faqs.question: What does a chemical peel actually do to my skin?
faqs.answer: A chemical peel applies an acidic solution that breaks the bonds between old, dead skin cells, causing controlled exfoliation. The removal of the outer skin layers triggers the skin's natural renewal response — stimulating cell turnover, new collagen production, and the emergence of fresh, undamaged skin from beneath. The result is brighter, smoother, more even-toned skin.

faqs.question: Is a chemical peel safe for darker skin tones?
faqs.answer: Yes — with the right peel selection. Higher-strength TCA peels and Jessner's peels carry risk of post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (PIH) in darker skin types. At Star Aesthetic, we use the mesoestetic mesopeel® range which includes specific formulations optimised for Fitzpatrick III–VI skin — including mandelic acid peels, which are preferred for darker skin due to their larger molecular size and lower PIH risk.

faqs.question: How long does the peeling last and what does it look like?
faqs.answer: Peeling typically begins 2–4 days after treatment and lasts 3–7 days, depending on peel depth. Skin sheds in patches — it may look like dry, flaking skin rather than dramatic sheets of peeling. This is the natural process of old, damaged skin cells shedding to reveal fresh skin beneath. Do not forcibly peel or pick — let it shed naturally.

faqs.question: Can I wear makeup after a chemical peel?
faqs.answer: Avoid all makeup for the first 24 hours, and only mineral makeup for days 2–5. During the active peeling phase, it is best to allow skin to breathe. Once peeling is complete (typically day 5–7), normal makeup use can resume.

faqs.question: What can I not do after a chemical peel?
faqs.answer: For 7–14 days post-peel: no direct sun exposure, no swimming pools (chlorine), no saunas, no strenuous exercise (heat increases redness and swelling), no active skincare actives (retinol, AHAs, BHAs, Vitamin C — unless directed otherwise). SPF50+ is mandatory every morning.

faqs.question: Which is better — a chemical peel or Dermapen microneedling?
faqs.answer: Different tools for different goals. Chemical peels excel at improving surface texture, pigmentation, and skin tone through exfoliation and cell renewal. Dermapen excels at stimulating deeper collagen production for wrinkle reduction, scar treatment, and skin density improvement. Many patients benefit from alternating both treatments throughout the year for comprehensive skin rejuvenation.

faqs.question: Will a chemical peel help my acne?
faqs.answer: Salicylic acid peels are particularly effective for acne-prone skin — they penetrate into pores to dissolve sebum and keratin plugs, reducing congestion and breakouts. Glycolic and mandelic acid peels also improve acne through accelerated cell turnover. Multiple sessions are needed for lasting improvement.

faqs.question: How many peels do I need?
faqs.answer: A course of 3–6 peels (spaced 2–4 weeks apart) is recommended for meaningful improvement in most skin concerns. A single peel provides a refreshing result but sustained improvement requires a series. Dr. Bangalee will recommend the appropriate number for your specific concern.

faqs.question: Is there any peel I can have done and still go to work the next day?
faqs.answer: Yes — superficial glycolic or mandelic acid peels (lower concentrations) cause only mild redness and minimal to no peeling. Most patients return to work the next day looking 'refreshed' rather than having visible peel signs. These are sometimes called 'lunch-hour peels.'

faqs.question: Can peels help with open pores?
faqs.answer: Yes — regular chemical peels, particularly salicylic acid and glycolic acid peels, reduce the appearance of enlarged pores by clearing congestion and stimulating the collagen that supports the pore walls. Combined with a retinol home-care programme (skinretin 0.3%), pore refinement over 3–6 months is significant and lasting.

title: Varicose Veins Treatment
tagline: Non-Surgical Thread Vein & Varicose Vein Management in Durban
quickSummary: Sclerotherapy — the injection of a sclerosing agent directly into unwanted veins — is the gold standard non-surgical treatment for spider veins (telangiectasias) and small-to-medium varicose veins, causing them to collapse and fade over 4–12 weeks.
downtime: Minimal — compression stockings worn for 5–7 days
downtimeDetail: Compression stockings for 5–7 days (worn continuously for the first 48 hours, then during the day). Avoid swimming, hot baths, saunas, and sunbathing for 2 weeks post-treatment. Walking is encouraged — avoid prolonged standing or sitting.
suitableFor: Adults with cosmetically bothersome spider veins or thread veins on the legsThose with small to medium varicose veins causing cosmetic distress or mild symptomsPatients experiencing leg aching, heaviness, or night cramps associated with visible vein diseaseThose who have been assessed and are not surgical candidates or prefer a non-surgical approachWomen post-pregnancy where varicose veins have developed or worsened
heroText: Varicose and spider veins affect approximately 1 in 3 adults and can cause both cosmetic distress and physical symptoms — aching, heaviness, swelling, and cramping in the legs. At Star Aesthetic Medical Centre, Dr. Bangalee offers non-surgical assessment and treatment options for thread veins and smaller varicose veins.
whatIs: Varicose veins are enlarged, twisted veins that result from failed valves in the venous system — blood pools in the vein rather than flowing efficiently back to the heart, causing the vein to dilate and become visible through the skin. Spider veins (telangiectasias) are smaller, superficial vessels visible as red, purple, or blue patterns.Sclerotherapy is the injection of a sclerosing (irritating) solution directly into the unwanted vein. The solution damages the inner lining of the vessel, causing it to collapse, stick together, and gradually be absorbed by the body over 4–12 weeks — leaving the skin clear where the vein once was.Dr. Bangalee performs a thorough vascular assessment before treatment to ensure that the veins being treated are appropriate for sclerotherapy and that there is no significant underlying venous insufficiency that requires referral to a vascular surgeon.
howWorks: Vascular Assessment — Dr. Bangalee assesses your veins clinically (and with Duplex ultrasound referral if indicated) to determine the extent of venous disease and suitability for sclerotherapyTreatment Mapping — The veins to be treated are identified and marked with the patient standingInjection — Using a very fine needle, a sclerosing solution is injected directly into each targeted vein. Multiple injections are typically performed per sessionCompression Application — Following treatment, graduated compression stockings are applied immediately and worn continuously for 5–7 days to optimise outcomeFollow-Up — Results are assessed at 4–6 weeks, and further sessions are planned as needed
expectedResults: Week 1–2: Treated veins appear inflamed and possibly darker — this is the inflammatory response and is expected. Weeks 3–6: Veins begin to fade as they are absorbed. Weeks 6–12: Progressive fading continues; treated veins largely or completely invisible. Multiple sessions required for extensive veins.

faqs.question: Does sclerotherapy hurt?
faqs.answer: Each injection causes a brief sting and a 2–3 second cramp-like sensation as the solution enters the vein. Most patients find this very tolerable. The procedure takes 30–60 minutes and most patients return to normal activities immediately.

faqs.question: How many sessions will I need?
